name: Carotid Atherosclerosis - Antiplatelet + Statin Query
description: Tests ability to find CAAD patients via medication codes — antiplatelets (aspirin, clopidogrel) and statins (atorvastatin). Heavy cross-indication with CHD/PAD — same drug class is used for any atherosclerotic vascular disease. This test will over-recall against the broader cardiovascular population.
prompt: Find patients prescribed antiplatelet medications (aspirin or clopidogrel) or atorvastatin for cardiovascular risk reduction.

prompts.naive: Find patients on antiplatelets and statins.
prompts.broad: Find patients prescribed aspirin, clopidogrel, or atorvastatin — antiplatelet and statin therapy commonly used for atherosclerotic vascular disease prevention.
prompts.expert: Identify patients treated pharmacologically for cardiovascular risk reduction consistent with carotid atherosclerosis via their medication orders. Search MedicationRequest resources for the antiplatelet agents — aspirin and clopidogrel — and the statin atorvastatin, all coded in RxNorm. Be aware that these medications are heavily cross-indicated across coronary heart disease, peripheral arterial disease, and carotid artery disease, so a medication-only cohort will substantially over-recall against the broader atherosclerotic vascular population.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients on antiplatelet/statin therapy. Heavy cross-indication with CHD/PAD.
